name: ETH Inventory Reset Reclaim v1
edge_mechanism: inventory-reset continuation after flush-and-reclaim rather than static oversold bounce logic
thesis: ETH 4h is the best similar-asset lane for testing whether a sharp flush that resets positioning can create a continuation edge only after price reclaims fast value and stabilizes. The edge is the repair-and-go move after inventory reset, not a blind oversold bounce.
invalidation_condition: Abort if flush-and-reclaim events remain too sparse, or if reclaimed resets repeatedly stall before producing enough continuation after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A sharp flush resets positioning, price reclaims value, and then continuation follows once the market proves the reset did not become a trend break.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Inventory flushes can force weak hands out. If value is reclaimed, the subsequent continuation can be stronger than a simple oversold bounce because positioning has already been reset.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is a clean lane for this because flush-and-reclaim structure is visible and not overly dependent on noisy intraday microstructure.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Some reclaims may only produce dead-cat bounces and fail before continuation develops.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If density is acceptable but moves stall early, shorten exits before altering the reclaim grammar.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A reasonable set of reset-and-go trades with first target doing most of the heavy lifting and occasional runner persistence.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: ETH 4h plus one cross-lane show credible density and positive continuation after reclaim.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sparse mirage, or repeated reclaims that fail to continue.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; flush, reclaim, and trend-break avoidance are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broader reset grammar rather than exact capitulation candle requirements

test_lanes.why_included: Best lane for flush-and-reclaim continuation on a liquid major.
test_lanes.continue_if: Reclaims occur often enough and continue after costs.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: The reset-and-go shape is too sparse or stalls early.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Sharp flushes that reclaim value and continue into a repair leg.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the same reset logic survives on BTC.
test_lanes.continue_if: BTC preserves the reclaim continuation shape with acceptable density.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Comparable but slightly cleaner reset continuations.

test_lanes.why_included: Tests whether the same logic survives on a more explosive lane without becoming pure noise.
test_lanes.continue_if: TAO still shows structured reset reclaims.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Fewer but larger reclaim continuation bursts.
